data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify's system is built on a microservices architecture, allowing independent deployment and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Each component of the application is a separate service, communicating over REST and gRPC.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Spotify utilizes load balancing to distribute incoming traffic across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Employs AWS Elastic Load Balancer and custom load balancing strategies.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A CDN is used to improve media streaming speeds by caching content closer to users.
data.technical_spec: Uses providers like CloudFront to handle content distribution globally.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: Incorporates event-driven processes to handle asynchronous tasks and scale operations.
data.technical_spec: Implements Apache Kafka to manage message queues and event streams.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Uses various databases for different data requirements, including NoSQL and SQL solutions.
data.technical_spec: Employs Cassandra, PostgreSQL, and other database systems.
